title: Heleniastrum tinctorium
intro: Heleniastrum tinctorium, also known as Indian gentian or yellow gentian, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of India and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Heleniastrum tinctorium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powder, often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Heleniastrum tinctorium is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. The genus Heleniastrum is a small group of plants native to the Mediterranean region, and Heleniastrum tinctorium is one of its species. It is also known as blue spurge or blue weed due to its bright blue flowers and ability to produce a spurge-like sap. The plant's classification is further characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and growth habit. As a member of the Boraginaceae family, Heleniastrum tinctorium shares characteristics with other plants like borage and forget-me-not.

parts: Heleniastrum tinctorium, also known as alkanna, is a plant that has been used for medicinal purposes in traditional medicine.
